articles.title: Government Says No to High Oil Prices
articles.summary: The government is telling energy companies to stop making too much money from high oil prices. Ed Miliband, the Energy Secretary, says they will not allow companies to charge too much for petrol and heating oil. Oil prices have gone up because of a war in Iran, making bills higher for people. The government might help by keeping fuel taxes low. Some companies say they need to raise prices, but the government wants to focus on clean energy like nuclear power to make energy cheaper and safer in the future. They are also talking to petrol sellers to make sure prices are fair.

articles.title: UK Economy Stays the Same in January as People Eat Out Less
articles.summary: In January, the UK economy did not grow at all, which was a surprise because experts thought it would grow a little. People spent less money eating at restaurants, which hurt the economy. The Office for National Statistics said the economy is quiet right now. The Prime Minister warned that a war between the US, Israel, and Iran could make things worse by raising fuel prices. This might make inflation go up, which is when prices for things like food and gas increase. If inflation rises, the Bank of England might not lower interest rates, which are costs for borrowing money. This could affect mortgages, making it harder for people to buy homes. The government says they have a plan to help by cutting living costs and debt, but others think more needs to be done to protect the economy.

articles.title: A Surprising Discovery from a Train Accident
articles.summary: Lauren Macpherson was on a train after a fun music festival when a heavy suitcase fell on her head. She went to the hospital for a check-up, and doctors found something unexpected in her brain. They told her she has a serious brain cancer that cannot be cured, and she might live for about 10 to 12 years. Before this, Lauren had been feeling very tired and had other health problems, but she didn't know why. The accident helped her find out about the cancer, which was both scary and a little relieving because she finally understood what was wrong. This story shows how accidents can lead to important discoveries about our health.

articles.title: A U.S. Military Plane Crashes in Iraq
articles.summary: A U.S. military refueling plane crashed in Iraq during an operation against Iran. The crash happened on a Thursday, and rescue efforts are ongoing to help any crew members. The plane was a KC-135 aircraft, and it had at least five people on board. The military says the crash was not caused by enemy or friendly fire. Another plane was involved but landed safely. This is the fourth plane crash in recent U.S. operations against Iran. Last week, three fighter jets were accidentally shot down by friendly fire from Kuwait, but all crew members were safely rescued and are doing okay. The military is asking for patience as they gather more details to inform the families.

articles.title: Fun Facts About the Oscars: Movies and Records
articles.summary: This article shares 17 fun facts about the Oscars, which are awards for movies. It talks about a movie called Sinners that mixes blues music and vampire horror, and it might win awards. Another movie, Zootopia 2, made a lot of money worldwide. Emma Stone broke records by being the youngest woman with seven Oscar nominations and having her first five nominations in movies that were also up for best picture. There's also a fact about a movie based on a very old book called Frankenstein. The Oscars are happening soon, and it's exciting to see which movies and people win.

articles.title: The Amazing Neon Artist: Wil Kirkman
articles.summary: This article is about Wil Kirkman, a talented artist who made beautiful neon signs. Neon signs are colorful glass lights that used to be very common but are now less seen. Wil lived in Boise, Idaho, and was famous for creating and fixing these delicate glass lights. He passed away from cancer last year, but before that, a reporter named Marcia Franklin watched him work. Wil loved making neon art, like a butterfly with blue, purple, and gold colors. He used special gases like neon and argon to make the lights glow. Wil described his work as a fun dance with fire and glass, where he felt happy and focused. The article shows how people are starting to appreciate this special art form again.
